commit: 07fcaa6 (+ delete_by_filter rebaselined to GHA value post-Mission-3 / B3)
captured_from: GHA runs 24213258406 + 24213724490 (same merge commit 07fcaa6, fifteen minutes apart). Non-noise-bound workloads use run 24213258406 values; six sub-millisecond aggregation/point-probe workloads (agg_sum/avg/min/max, powql_aggregation, point_lookup_nonindexed) use max(run1, run2) values because the two runs produced +14 to +31% spread on those workloads with no code change between them — runner-variance, not regression. See compare.rs::VERY_NOISY_ABSOLUTE_THRESHOLD for the widened threshold tier that routes those six workloads at +/-20%. delete_by_filter was rebaselined from 152141 → 171061 ns on the mission-parallel-integration branch (PR #8 GHA run 24225043310): Mission 3 (persistent btree indexes) added a ~12% cost on the GHA x86-64-v2 runner to indexed delete paths even after Blocker B3 deferred per-row btree.save fsyncs to checkpoint time. The trade-off is intentional — Mission 3 makes secondary indexes crash-safe, which is a load-bearing durability feature for production use, and the rebaseline is the residual after B3 already recovered most of a 33x raw regression. All other 19 workloads are within tolerance.
note: Per-workload absolute gate is +/-7% default (storage + legacy PowQL), +/-10% for noisy write/sort workloads, and +/-20% for the six Azure-pool-variance-dominated aggregation/point workloads (see compare.rs::threshold_for). The massive regression shown against the OLD btree_lookup=1832 and seq_scan_filter=13738549 numbers (btree -85.68%, seq_scan_filter -51.70%) is genuine improvement from Mission D (read fast paths) + Mission F (LTO), NOT noise — the pre-Mission-D baseline was captured before cross-crate inlining and the compiled-predicate scan walker landed. btree_lookup dropped disproportionately (7x) vs powql_point (2.87x), which strains the powql_point_over_btree_lookup thesis ratio — see thesis-ratios.json for the relax commentary.
